title: Recovering a Deleted Branch
description: A colleague deleted the branch `feat/search` thinking it had been merged. It wasn't. The commits exist in the reflog. You need to bring them back.
objective: A branch named `feat/search` must exist and must contain the three commits that were on it before deletion. The search feature code must be present, `HEAD` must be attached to a named branch, and the branch must be in a clean state.
hints: `git reflog` shows the history of HEAD movements and branch updates.
Use `git reflog refs/heads/feat/search` or `git reflog --all` to find the last known SHA of `feat/search` after deletion.
`git checkout -b feat/search <sha>` recreates the branch at the right point.
